message:
🌱 Add operator-framework: Kubebuilder is not listed in prerequisites  mission (#1618)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,77 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "operator-framework-3461-kubebuilder-is-not-listed-in-prerequisites",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "operator-framework: Kubebuilder is not listed in prerequisites ",
+    "description": "Kubebuilder is not listed in prerequisites . Requested by 9+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current operator-framework deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your operator-framework version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n operator-framework -l app.kubernetes.io/name=operator-framework
helm list -n operator-framework 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working operator-framework installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review operator-framework configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant operator-framework configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n operator-framework -l app.kubernetes.io/name=operator-framework
kubectl get configmap -n operator-framework -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=operator-framework
```
Kubebuilder is Tightly coupled in requirements, which is fine but...
1. `kubebuilder` is not listed in [Prerequisites](https://sdk.operatorframework.io/docs/golang/quickstart/#prerequisites)
2."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Kubebuilder is not listed in prerequisites ",
+        "description": "- Add info in the quick start over how to setup the env test. Otherwise. it will fail when users run `docker-build`
- Add new doc with its options and more detailed information in the references.

- https://github.com/operator-framework/operator-sdk/issues/3461
```yaml
Failure [0.002 seconds]
[BeforeSuite] BeforeSuite 
/home/deepak/projects/go/src/operator/newoperator3/final/crda-operator/controllers/suite_test.go:52

  Unexpected error:
      <*fmt.wrapError | 0xc00046c2a0>: {
          msg: "failed to start the controlplane. retried 5 times: fork/exec /usr/local/kubebuilder/bin/etcd: no such file or directory",
          err: {
              Op: "fork/exec",
              Path: "/usr/local/kubebuilder/bin/etcd",
              Err: 0x2,
          },
      }
      failed to start the controlplane. retried 5 times: fork/exec /usr/local/kubebuilder/bin/etcd: n
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n operator-framework -l app.kubernetes.io/name=operator-framework
kubectl get events -n operator-framework --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Kubebuilder is not listed in prerequisites " is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "- Add info in the quick start over how to setup the env test. Otherwise. it will fail when users run `docker-build`
- Add new doc with its options and more detailed information in the references.

- https://github.com/operator-framework/operator-sdk/issues/3461",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "Failure [0.002 seconds]
[BeforeSuite] BeforeSuite 
/home/deepak/projects/go/src/operator/newoperator3/final/crda-operator/controllers/suite_test.go:52

  Unexpected error:
      <*fmt.wrapError | 0xc00046c2a0>: {
          msg: "failed to start the controlplane. retried 5 times: fork/exec /usr/local/kubebuilder/bin/etcd: no such file or directory",
          err: {
              Op: "fork/exec",
              Path: "/usr/local/kubebuilder/bin/etcd",
              Err: 0x2,
          },

      failed to start the controlplane. retried 5 times: fork/exec /usr/local/kubebuilder/bin/etcd: no such file or directory
  occurred

------------------------------


Ran 0 of 0 Specs in 0.002 se",
+        "ENVTEST_ASSETS_DIR=$(pwd)/test/assets
fetch_envtest_assets() {
    OS=$(uname -s | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')
    ARCH=$(uname -m | sed 's/x86_64/amd64/')
    mkdir -p $ENVTEST_ASSETS_DIR
    curl -fsL "https://storage.googleapis.com/kubebuilder-tools/kubebuilder-tools-1.16.4-${OS}-${ARCH}.tar.gz" | tar zx --strip-components=1 -C $ENVTEST_ASSETS_DIR
}
setup_envtest_env() {
    export TEST_ASSET_KUBE_APISERVER=$ENVTEST_ASSETS_DIR/bin/kube-apiserver
    export TEST_ASSET_ETCD=$ENVTEST_ASSETS_DIR/bin/etcd
    export TEST_ASSET_KUBECTL=$ENVTEST_ASSETS_DIR/bin/kubectl
}",
+        "> 
> to your Makefile, what happens?

Thanks Estroz. I was experiencing the same problem as the OP. Doing your suggestion solved the problem!

Environment - 
Kali linux WSL2 instance. 
I'm still experiencing this issue
OS: WSL2

Still does not work!

Commit - https://github.com/evergreen-trading-systems/yta-operator/commit/64a13f71d2b05bc45e4285e6942e3249af85790a#diff-76ed074a9305c04054cdebb9e9aad2d818052b07091de1f20cad0bbac34ffb52

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/4870868/126915497-aef48f14-19b2-46d8-b010-8071e5e336b3.png)


Yep, I think this is a user-experience issue. If I have to do some research to rename a path for kubebuilder manually through some riga-ma"
